speaker: POLONIUS
text: Give him this money and these notes, Reynaldo.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You shall do marvelous wisely, good Reynaldo,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Before you visit him, to make inquire

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Of his behavior.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: My lord, I did intend it.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Marry, well said, very well said. Look you, sir,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Inquire me first what Danskers are in Paris;

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And how, and who, what means, and where they keep,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What company, at what expense; and finding

speaker: POLONIUS
text: By this encompassment and drift of question

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That they do know my son, come you more nearer

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Than your particular demands will touch it.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Take you, as ’twere, some distant knowledge of him,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As thus: “I know his father and his friends

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And, in part, him.” Do you mark this, Reynaldo?

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Ay, very well, my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: “And, in part, him, but,” you may say, “not well.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But if ’t be he I mean, he’s very wild,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Addicted so and so.” And there put on him

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What forgeries you please—marry, none so rank

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As may dishonor him, take heed of that,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But, sir, such wanton, wild, and usual slips

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As are companions noted and most known

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To youth and liberty.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: As gaming, my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Ay, or drinking, fencing, swearing,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Quarreling, drabbing—you may go so far.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: My lord, that would dishonor him.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Faith, no, as you may season it in the charge.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You must not put another scandal on him

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That he is open to incontinency;

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That’s not my meaning. But breathe his faults so quaintly

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That they may seem the taints of liberty,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The flash and outbreak of a fiery mind,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: A savageness in unreclaimèd blood,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Of general assault.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: But, my good lord—

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Wherefore should you do this?

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Ay, my lord, I would know that.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Marry, sir, here’s my drift,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And I believe it is a fetch of wit.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You, laying these slight sullies on my son,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As ’twere a thing a little soiled i’ th’ working,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Mark you, your party in converse, him you would sound,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Having ever seen in the prenominate crimes

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The youth you breathe of guilty, be assured

speaker: POLONIUS
text: He closes with you in this consequence:

speaker: POLONIUS
text: “Good sir,” or so, or “friend,” or “gentleman,”

speaker: POLONIUS
text: According to the phrase or the addition

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Of man and country—

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Very good, my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And then, sir, does he this, he does—what

speaker: POLONIUS
text: was I about to say? By the Mass, I was about to say

speaker: POLONIUS
text: something. Where did I leave?

speaker: REYNALDO
text: At “closes in the consequence,” at “friend,

speaker: REYNALDO
text: or so,” and “gentleman.”

speaker: POLONIUS
text: At “closes in the consequence”—ay, marry—

speaker: POLONIUS
text: He closes thus: “I know the gentleman.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I saw him yesterday,” or “th’ other day”

speaker: POLONIUS
text: (Or then, or then, with such or such), “and as you say,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: There was he gaming, there o’ertook in ’s rouse,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: There falling out at tennis”; or perchance

speaker: POLONIUS
text: “I saw him enter such a house of sale”—

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Videlicet, a brothel—or so forth. See you now

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Your bait of falsehood take this carp of truth;

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And thus do we of wisdom and of reach,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: With windlasses and with assays of bias,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: By indirections find directions out.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: So by my former lecture and advice

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Shall you my son. You have me, have you not?

speaker: REYNALDO
text: My lord, I have.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: God be wi’ you. Fare you well.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Good my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Observe his inclination in yourself.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: I shall, my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And let him ply his music.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Well, my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Farewell.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: How now, Ophelia, what’s the matter?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: O, my lord, my lord, I have been so affrighted!

speaker: POLONIUS
text: With what, i’ th’ name of God?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My lord, as I was sewing in my closet,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Lord Hamlet, with his doublet all unbraced,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: No hat upon his head, his stockings fouled,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Ungartered, and down-gyvèd to his ankle,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Pale as his shirt, his knees knocking each other,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And with a look so piteous in purport

speaker: OPHELIA
text: As if he had been loosèd out of hell

speaker: OPHELIA
text: To speak of horrors—he comes before me.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Mad for thy love?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My lord, I do not know,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: But truly I do fear it.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What said he?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He took me by the wrist and held me hard.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Then goes he to the length of all his arm,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And, with his other hand thus o’er his brow,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He falls to such perusal of my face

speaker: OPHELIA
text: As he would draw it. Long stayed he so.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: At last, a little shaking of mine arm,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And thrice his head thus waving up and down,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He raised a sigh so piteous and profound

speaker: OPHELIA
text: As it did seem to shatter all his bulk

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And end his being. That done, he lets me go,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And, with his head over his shoulder turned,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He seemed to find his way without his eyes,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: For out o’ doors he went without their helps

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And to the last bended their light on me.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Come, go with me. I will go seek the King.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: This is the very ecstasy of love,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Whose violent property fordoes itself

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And leads the will to desperate undertakings

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As oft as any passions under heaven

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That does afflict our natures. I am sorry.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What, have you given him any hard words of late?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: No, my good lord, but as you did command

speaker: OPHELIA
text: I did repel his letters and denied

speaker: OPHELIA
text: His access to me.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That hath made him mad.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I am sorry that with better heed and judgment

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I had not coted him. I feared he did but trifle

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And meant to wrack thee. But beshrew my jealousy!

speaker: POLONIUS
text: By heaven, it is as proper to our age

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To cast beyond ourselves in our opinions

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As it is common for the younger sort

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To lack discretion. Come, go we to the King.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: This must be known, which, being kept close, might move

speaker: POLONIUS
text: More grief to hide than hate to utter love.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Come.
